text: A database is an organized collection of information that can be easily accessed and managed. In SQL, or relational databases, data is stored in tables with rows and columns, similar to a spreadsheet. To extract specific information, you write queries using SQL language. Data modeling is the process of designing these tables and defining how they relate to each other. To speed up searches, you can create indexes on columns. NoSQL databases, on the other hand, store data in different structures like documents or key-value pairs, which are more flexible and suitable for large, unstructured datasets. The choice between SQL and NoSQL depends on your project's needs for consistency or scalability.
